False  

The output will be `{'b': 4}` because the dictionary comprehension doubles the value (`v * 2`) but only includes items where the original value is greater than 1 (`if v > 1`). Since 'a' has value 1, it's excluded, and only 'b' with value 2 gets doubled to 4.  

(But I'm a bit confused why the original dictionary had 'a':1 and 'b':2 - was that important for the condition?)
